content.problemStatement:
You are given a **binary** string AAA of length NNN. The decimal representation of this string could be huge.

Your task is to find out two binary strings BBB and CCC, each of length NNN, such that:


(A)10=(B)10−(C)10(A)_{10} = (B)_{10} - (C)_{10}(A)10​=(B)10​−(C)10​;
The total count of 111s in BBB and CCC is **minimum**.
If multiple such strings BBB and CCC exist, you can print any.

It can be proven that at least one solution always exists.

Note: (X)10(X)_{10}(X)10​ represents the decimal representation of binary string XXX.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the length of the binary string AAA.
The following line has the binary string AAA of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output binary strings BBB and CCC, each of length NNN, on separate lines.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1055\cdot 10^{5}5⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: (A)10=1⋅20+1⋅21+1⋅22+0⋅23+0⋅24=7(A)_{10} = 1\cdot 2^{0} + 1\cdot 2^{1} + 1\cdot 2^{2} + 0\cdot 2^{3} + 0\cdot 2^{4} = 7(A)10​=1⋅20+1⋅21+1⋅22+0⋅23+0⋅24=7.
Consider B=(01000)2≡(8)10B = (01000)_2 \equiv (8)_{10}B=(01000)2​≡(8)10​ and C=(00001)2≡(1)10C = (00001)_2 \equiv (1)_{10}C=(00001)2​≡(1)10​. Here,

(A)10=(B)10−(C)10(A)_{10} = (B)_{10} - (C)_{10}(A)10​=(B)10​−(C)10​;
The total count of 111s in BBB and CCC is 222.

It can be shown that there exist no other values of BBB and CCC which satisfy the condition and have a smaller total count of 222.
Test case 222: (A)10=0⋅20+1⋅21=2(A)_{10} = 0\cdot 2^{0} + 1\cdot 2^{1} = 2(A)10​=0⋅20+1⋅21=2.
Consider B=(10)2≡(2)10B = (10)_2 \equiv (2)_{10}B=(10)2​≡(2)10​ and C=(00)2≡(0)10C = (00)_2 \equiv (0)_{10}C=(00)2​≡(0)10​. Here,

(A)10=(B)10−(C)10(A)_{10} = (B)_{10} - (C)_{10}(A)10​=(B)10​−(C)10​;
The total count of 111s in BBB and CCC is 111.

It can be shown that there exist no other values of BBB and CCC which satisfy the condition and have a smaller total count of 111.
